 {"id":"bd-139x","title":"Phase 7: Performance Conformance (load time and dispatch latency)","description":"# Phase 7: Performance Conformance (load time and dispatch latency)

## Purpose
Not just correctness -- we need competitive performance. The Rust runtime should be FASTER than the TypeScript runtime, or at worst within 2x.

## Metrics

### Extension Load Time
- Measure: time from loadExtension() call to Extension object available
- Target: Rust <= 2x TypeScript for any extension
- Stretch goal: Rust <= 1x TypeScript (faster than TS)
- Method: both harnesses report load_time_ms in their JSON output

### Event Dispatch Latency
- Measure: time from event fire to handler response received
- Target: Rust < 5ms p99 for all event types
- Method: fire 1000 events per type, measure each

### Memory Usage
- Measure: RSS increase from loading N extensions
- Target: Rust <= 1.5x TypeScript per extension
- Method: measure process RSS before/after loading

### Concurrent Extension Stress
- Load 10+ extensions simultaneously
- Fire events to all extensions concurrently
- Verify no deadlocks, no data races, no memory leaks
- Run for 1 hour continuously

## Implementation
- Load time: already captured by differential runner
- Event dispatch: add timing to event fire/response in both harnesses
- Memory: separate benchmark test with RSS monitoring
- Stress: dedicated stress test binary

## Acceptance Criteria
- Load time benchmarks for all 60 official extensions
- Event dispatch p99 < 5ms verified
- Memory growth < 10MB per extension loaded
- 1-hour stress test passes without degradation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:53.762589183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:39:40.110001790Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:39:40.109850227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-139x","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:53.773572100Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-13gm","title":"Task: Implement agent lifecycle events (startup, agent_start, agent_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Agent Lifecycle Events

## Objective

Dispatch startup, agent_start, and agent_end events at agent lifecycle boundaries.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// At agent initialization
if (runner.hasHandlers('startup')) {
    await runner.emit({ type: 'startup', version: PI_VERSION, sessionFile });
}

// At start of agent.run()
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_start')) {
    await runner.emit({ type: 'agent_start', sessionId: session.id });
}

// At end of agent.run() (including errors)
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        messages: conversation.messages,
        error: error?.message,
    });
}
```

## Events Covered

### startup Event
- **When**: Agent process initialization
- **Purpose**: One-time setup, version check
- **Data**: version, session_file

### agent_start Event  
- **When**: Before first API call in a run
- **Purpose**: Initialize resources, modify system prompt
- **Data**: session_id

### agent_end Event
- **When**: After agent loop ends (success or error)
- **Purpose**: Cleanup, logging, analytics
- **Data**: session_id, messages, error (if any)

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<AgentResult> {
        let dispatcher = self.extension_manager.as_ref().map(|em| em.event_dispatcher());
        
        // Dispatch agent_start
        if let Some(d) = &dispatcher {
            if d.has_handlers("agent_start") {
                d.dispatch::<()>(ExtensionEvent::AgentStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                }).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Run agent loop
        let result = self.run_loop(&dispatcher).await;
        
        // Dispatch agent_end (even on error)
        if let Some(d) = &dispatcher {
            if d.has_handlers("agent_end") {
                let _ = d.dispatch::<()>(ExtensionEvent::AgentEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    messages: self.session.get_messages(),
                    error: result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string()),
                }).await;
            }
        }
        
        result
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (6 cases)
1. test_startup_fires_on_init
2. test_agent_start_fires_before_loop
3. test_agent_end_fires_after_success
4. test_agent_end_fires_after_error
5. test_agent_end_includes_messages
6. test_agent_end_includes_error_message

### E2E Test Script
Extension tracking full agent lifecycle with JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] startup dispatched on init
- [ ] agent_start dispatched at run start
- [ ] agent_end dispatched at run end (always)
- [ ] Error included in agent_end when failed
- [ ] 6 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:31.639958959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:01:56.958694846Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:01:56.958630015Z","close_reason":"Agent lifecycle events (startup, agent_start, agent_end) fully implemented in src/agent.rs. startup hook fires at agent init (line ~2593). AgentStart dispatched before first loop (line ~567). AgentEnd dispatched at all exit paths with error info included. Extension dispatch via dispatch_extension_lifecycle_event is fail-open.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:31.649182507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:49.116039181Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:48.170234734Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-141p","title":"Create deterministic test environment for both runtimes","description":"# Create deterministic test environment for both runtimes

## Context
For differential testing to work, BOTH runtimes must produce identical outputs for the same inputs. This requires eliminating all sources of non-determinism:
- Timestamps (Date.now(), new Date())
- Random values (Math.random())
- File paths (cwd, home directory)
- Process IDs, hostnames
- Network responses (must be mocked)
- Filesystem state (must be sandboxed)

## What To Do
1. For the TS runtime: create wrapper that patches Date, Math.random, process.cwd, etc.
2. For the Rust runtime: ensure QuickJS globals are patched similarly
3. Both runtimes must use identical mock values for:
   - Current time: fixed epoch (e.g., 1700000000000)
   - Random seed: fixed (e.g., always returns 0.5)
   - CWD: /tmp/ext-conformance-test
   - Home: /tmp/ext-conformance-home
4. Document all patched globals

## Why This Matters
Without deterministic environments, outputs will differ due to timestamps alone. Every comparison would be noisy with false positives. Determinism is a PREREQUISITE for meaningful differential testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical JSON for a simple extension that uses Date.now()
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical JSON for an extension that uses Math.random()
- CWD and home dir paths are normalized in both outputs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:59.950761840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:21.002009680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:21.001936804Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic globals for TS+Rust harnesses and added deterministic diff test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-141p","depends_on_id":"bd-1v10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:59.975032304Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":406,"issue_id":"bd-141p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed compilation error: Changed double quotes to single quotes in JS string literals inside PI_BRIDGE_JS raw string (lines 4341, 4368-4371). The r"..." raw string delimiter requires single quotes inside JS code. Build now passes and all 60 official conformance tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:43:58Z"},{"id":407,"issue_id":"bd-141p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deterministic test infrastructure verified: diff_deterministic_globals test passes. Both TS and Rust runtimes produce identical outputs for Date.now(), Math.random(), process.cwd(), and process.env.HOME when PI_DETERMINISTIC_* env vars are set. The fixture extension tests/fixtures/determinism_extension.ts registers a tool with values embedded in name/description for easy verification.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:45:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-143c","title":"Epic: Theme System Completion","description":"Complete the theme system to 100% parity with legacy pi-mono.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-06)
- Theme discovery from directories: DONE
- JSON theme file loading + validation: DONE
- Theme switching via `/theme`: DONE
- Hot reload via `/reload`: DONE
- Built-in themes (dark, light, solarized): DONE
- CLI `--theme` flag support: DONE (`bd-2wue`)

## Remaining Work (parity-critical)
1. Theme picker UI in `/settings` modal + persistence (`bd-ancm`)

## Optional / Stretch
- Theme composition/inheritance only if required for legacy parity (otherwise explicitly out-of-scope).

## Success Criteria
- Users can pick themes via `/settings` UI.
- Selected theme persists in settings and is applied on startup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:04.568390134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:22:23.955843356Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:22:23.955776942Z","close_reason":"Theme picker UI + persistence completed (bd-ancm/bd-ieym closed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-143c","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:35:43.888152428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-147","title":"Write extension compatibility summary (goals, gaps, next steps)","description":"Background:
- We need a concise narrative of goals, choices, and known limitations.

Steps:
- Summarize the rationale for the sample and harness design.
- List known gaps or incompatible extensions with reasons.
- Provide a roadmap for closing remaining gaps.

Acceptance:
- Summary can serve as a handoff for future work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
